# How to Use Hiring Signals for Cold Email in 2026
**Channel:** Eric Nowoslawski
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s_EUgLx-esY
**Date:** 2026-03-31
**Content source:** auto-generated transcript

## Summary
Eric Nowoslawski argues that the traditional approach of simply mentioning that a company is hiring is outdated and ineffective for cold email in 2026. Instead, he recommends extracting deeper insights from job descriptions to craft more personalized, research-backed outreach that doesn't get lumped in with generic hiring-signal emails.

## Key Points & Strategies
- **Outdated signal approach:** Simply saying "I saw you're hiring for [role]" is now table stakes and doesn't differentiate your outreach
- **Deep insight extraction:** Mine job descriptions for the "gold" — the underlying business goals and context behind the hiring
- **Avoid getting written off:** Mentioning hiring signals directly triggers recipients to dismiss emails as generic; focus on the underlying insights instead
- **Examples of extracted insights:**
  - Hiring 5 enterprise AEs = company pursuing enterprise/upmarket expansion
  - Hiring CISOs + security engineers = need for SOC 2, compliance, security fortification
- **Prompt development methodology:** Review 10 relevant job descriptions manually, document what stands out, create AI prompts based on those patterns, then validate outputs before scaling

## Workflows & Tools Discussed
- **Job data sources:** Clay.com, Apify.com, Parallel.ai
- **Job scraping:** Apify's Indeed scraper, Clay's LinkedIn-integrated job data, LinkedIn Jobs API via PredictLeads
- **AI cost optimization:** Open Router (open-source models), Deep Infra, Vast.ai (GPU rental for high-volume processing)
- **Process:** Scrape jobs → Extract insights with AI → Manually write 3-10 sample emails based on insights → Train AI prompt with examples → Scale validated messaging

## Use Cases Covered
- SaaS companies targeting hiring signals
- E-commerce companies using hiring data
- Enterprise sales outreach based on hiring for enterprise AEs
- Security/compliance outreach based on security team hiring
- Outsourcing pitches (lead gen, managed services, design)

## Quotes Worth Noting
> "I hate this strategy... if somebody positively responds... either they just don't get a lot of email or they would have responded to your offer anyway. I don't think it helps that much."

> "Look in the job description. And AI makes this so ridiculously cheap to be able to look in the job description... you can look at why they're hiring for STRs and then parse through the gold that's in that job description instead."
